subject: Cloud Computing
title: The $600 Billion Market
description: A micro-lesson that makes The $600 Billion Market usable.

hook.text: Global cloud spending exceeded $600 billion in 2023. By 2030, forecasts predict it will double.

buildup.text: Cloud infrastructure, SaaS, and PaaS combined represent one of the fastest-growing sectors in tech.

discovery.text: Only about 30% of enterprise workloads are in the cloud today. The migration wave still has years to run.

twist.text: AI is accelerating cloud spending. Training and running AI models requires cloud-scale compute that no one owns.

climax.text: Cloud computing isn't a mature market. It's still in the growth phase, with decades of expansion ahead.

punchline.text: Six hundred billion dollars. And we're still early.

quiz.question: What percentage of enterprise workloads are currently in the cloud?
options: About 30%, with significant room for growth
Over 90%, with the migration nearly complete
Less than 5%, with most companies rejecting cloud
